# Chapter 6 Maintenance Console

Use the Maintenance Console to create and edit information pertaining to casino machines.


## 6.1 Accessing the Maintenance Console

To access the Maintenance Console:

Click Maintenance Console in the Quick Access toolbar OR navigate to IGT Spade >
Floor > Maintenance Console. The Maintenance Console window opens.




![Figure 6-1 Maintenance Console](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/69.2.png)




<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="69" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 6 Maintenance Console" -->


## 6.2 Cabinet Types

Use the Cabinet Types pane to manage cabinet types at the property.


### To view cabinet types:

1\. Select IGT Spade > Floor.

2\. Select Maintenance Console > Cabinet Types. The Cabinet Types pane appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/70.1.png)




#### 6.2.1 Adding Cabinet Types

To add a cabinet type:

1\. Click Add under the Cabinet Types pane. A new item appears.

2\. Enter the name of the new cabinet type in the New Item field.

3\. Press ENTER. The cabinet type appears in the Cabinet Type pane.


#### 6.2.2 Modifying Cabinet Types

To modify an existing cabinet type:

1\. Select a cabinet type to modify in the Cabinet Types pane, then click Modify.

2\. Edit the cabinet type name as necessary, then press ENTER. The Cabinet Type pane
refreshes with the changes.


#### 6.2.3 Deleting Cabinet Types

To delete a cabinet type:

1\. Select a cabinet type to delete in the Cabinet Types pane.

2\. Click Delete. The cabinet type no longer appears on the Cabinet Type pane.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="70" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 6 Maintenance Console" -->




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/71.1.png)




No confirmation window appears when deleting a cabinet type. Always confirm the
deletion of a cabinet type before selecting the action.

If the cabinet type does not delete, the following prompt appears: "The value you
are trying to delete is referenced by another table." If the cabinet type is being used,
it cannot be deleted.


## 6.3 Sections

Use the Sections pane to manage sections of the property's casino floor.

To view sections:

1\. Select IGT Spade > Floor.

2\. Select Maintenance Console > Sections. The Section Name pane appears.




![Figure 6-2 Maintenance Console-Sections](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/71.2.png)




### 6.3.1 Adding Sections

To add a section:

1\. Click Add in the Section Name pane. The Section Configuration window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/71.3.png)




2\. Enter up to four alphanumeric characters for the new section in the Section Name field.

3\. Click OK. The Section Name pane displays the new section.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="71" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 6 Maintenance Console" -->


### 6.3.2 Modifying Sections


#### To modify an existing section:

1\. Select a section to modify in the Section Name pane.

2\. Click Modify. The Section Configuration window appears.

3\. Edit the section name as needed.

4\. Click OK. The section name changes.


### 6.3.3 Deleting Sections

To delete a section:

1\. Select a section to delete in the Section Name pane.

2\. Click Delete. The section no longer appears on the Section Name pane.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/72.1.png)




No confirmation window appears when deleting a section. Always confirm a
section's deletion before selecting the action. If the section does not delete, the
following prompt appears: "The value you are trying to delete is referenced by
another table." A section cannot be deleted when in use.


## 6.4 Game Types

Use the Game Type pane to manage game types at the property.


### To view game types:

1\. Select IGT Spade > Floor.

2\. Select Maintenance Console > Game Types. The Game Type pane appears.




![Figure 6-3 Maintenance Console-Game Types](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/72.2.png)




<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="72" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 6 Maintenance Console" -->


## 6.4.1 Adding Game Types

To add a game type:

1\. Click Add under the Game Type pane. The New Item field appears.

2\. Enter the name of the new game type in the New Item field.

3\. Press ENTER. The Game Type pane displays the new game type.


## 6.4.2 Modifying Game Types

To modify an existing game type:

1\. Select a game type to modify in the Game Type pane.

2\. Click Modify. The game type becomes editable.

3\. Edit the game type name as needed.

4\. Press ENTER. The Game Type pane displays the updated game type.


## 6.4.3 Deleting Game Types

To delete a game type:

1\. Select a game type to delete in the Game Type pane.

2\. Click Delete. The Game Type pane no longer shows the deleted game type




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/73.1.png)




!
No confirmation window appears when deleting a game type. Always confirm the
deletion of a game type before selecting the action. If the game type does not delete,
the following prompt appears: "The value you are trying to delete is referenced by
another table."


## 6.5 Display Types

Use the Display Type pane to manage display types at the property.

To view display types:

1\. Select IGT Spade > Floor.

2\. Select Maintenance Console > Display Types. The Display Type pane appears.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="73" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 6 Maintenance Console" -->




![Figure 6-4 Maintenance Console-Display Type](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/74.1.png)




### 6.5.1 Adding Display Types


#### To add a display type:

1\. Click Add under the Display Type pane. A new display type appears.

2\. Enter the name of the new display type in the New Item field.

3\. Press ENTER. The Display Type pane displays the new display type.


### 6.5.2 Modifying Display Types


#### To modify an existing display type:

1\. Select a display type to modify in the Display Type pane.

2\. Click Modify. The display type becomes editable.

3\. Edit the display type name as needed.

4\. Press ENTER. The Display Type pane displays the display type's new name.


### 6.5.3 Deleting Display Types


#### To delete a display type:

1\. Select a display type to delete in the Display Type pane.

2\. Click Delete. The Display Type pane no longer displays the deleted display type.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/74.2.png)




No confirmation window appears when deleting a display type. Always confirm a
display type should be deleted before selecting the action. If the display type does
not delete, the following message appears: "The value you are trying to delete is
referenced by another table." A display type cannot be deleted when in use.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="74" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 6 Maintenance Console" -->


## 6.6 Manufacturers

Use the Manufacturers option to manage the manufacturers used at the property.

To view manufacturers:

1\. Select IGT Spade > Floor.

2\. Select Maintenance Console > Manufacturers. The Description pane appears.




![Figure 6-5 Maintenance Console-Manufacturers](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/75.1.png)




### 6.6.1 Adding Manufacturers

To add a manufacturer:

1\. Click Add under the Description pane. A new manufacturer appears in the Description
pane.

2\. Enter the new manufacturer's name in the New Item field.

3\. Press ENTER. The Description pane displays the new manufacturer.


### 6.6.2 Modifying Manufacturers


#### To modify an existing manufacturer:

1\. Select a manufacturer to modify in the Description pane.

2\. Click Modify. The manufacturer becomes editable.

3\. Edit the manufacturer's name as needed.

4\. Press ENTER. The Description pane displays the manufacturer's updated name.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="75" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 6 Maintenance Console" -->


### 6.6.3 Deleting Manufacturers

To delete a manufacturer:

1\. Select a manufacturer to delete in the Manufacturers window.

2\. Click Delete. The Description pane no longer displays the deleted manufacturer.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/76.1.png)




!
No confirmation window appears when deleting a manufacturer. Always confirm
the correct manufacturer is selected before selecting the action. If the manufacturer
does not delete, the following message appears: "The value you are trying to delete
is referenced by another table." A manufacturer cannot be deleted when in use.


## 6.7 Denominations

To view denominations:

Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Maintenance Console > Denominations. The

### 6.7.1 Adding Denominations

To add a denomination:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Maintenance Console > Denominations. The
Denominations pane appears.

2\. Click Add under the Denominations pane. The Enter Denomination Information window
appears.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="76" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 6 Maintenance Console" -->




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/77.1.png)




3\. Use the following table as a reference for configuring the denomination information.


<table>
<tr>
<th>Field</th>
<th>Description</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Denomination</td>
<td>Enter the new denomination.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Amount per Bag</td>
<td>Enter the amount of a denomination per fill bag.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Hard Drop Variance</td>
<td>Enter the hard drop variance (in dollars). O 1 Any difference between the metered and actual hard drop greater than the variance amount appears as a variance, which must be reconciled during the accounting process.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Soft Drop Variance</td>
<td>Enter the soft drop variance (in dollars). O 1 Any difference between the metered and actual soft drop greater than the variance amount appears as a variance, which must be reconciled during the accounting process.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Beginning Range</td>
<td>Enter the beginning asset number. 0 1 Machine Accounting uses blocks of asset numbers to group denominations (for example, 100000-199999 for one-cent machines). Added ranges may not overlap with existing ranges.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Ending Range</td>
<td>Enter the ending asset number.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Tolerance Level</td>
<td>Select a value from the drop-down list. Refer to Binding Tolerance Levels to Denominations on page 78 for more information.</td>
</tr>
</table>


4\. Click Save. The new denomination appears on the Denominations pane.


### 6.7.2 Modifying Denominations


#### To modify a denomination:

1\. Select a denomination to modify in the Denominations pane.

2\. Click Modify. The Enter Denomination Information window appears.

3\. Edit the fields as needed.

4\. Click OK. The denomination information updates.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="77" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 6 Maintenance Console" -->


### 6.7.3 Deleting Denominations

To delete a denomination:

1\. Select a denomination to delete in the Denominations window.

2\. Click Delete. The Denominations pane no longer shows the deleted denomination.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/78.1.png)




No confirmation window appears when deleting a denomination. Always confirm
the deletion of a denomination before selecting the action. If the denomination does
not delete, the following message appears: "The value you are trying to delete is
referenced by another table." Denominations in use cannot be deleted.


## 6.7.4 Binding Tolerance Levels to Denominations




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/78.2.png)




Refer to Tolerance Level on page 109 for more information.

Select a tolerance level in the Enter Denomination Information window (Maintenance
Console > System Maintenance > Denominations). Doing so binds the level to the
denomination. A tolerance level is not required.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/78.3.png)




For example, if a machine type has a denomination of 5.00, and 1-Level 1 is selected, the
default tolerance level comprises the same values as those defined for 1-Level 1 in the second
window of the Machine Type Setup Wizard. Refer to Machine Types

<!-- PageNumber="78" -->
<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 6 Maintenance Console" -->


![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/79.1.png)

## 6.8 Extended Denom Range

Use the Extended Denom Range options to extend the number of allowable machine asset
numbers for a specific denomination. Denom ranges can only be extended once, and cannot be
extended to overlap with any other current ranges.


### To view extended denom ranges:

Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Maintenance Console > Extended Denom Range.
The Extended Denom Range pane appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/80.1.png)




#### 6.8.1 Adding Extended Denom Ranges

To add an extended denom range:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Maintenance Console > Extended Denom
Range. The Extended Denom Range pane appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/80.2.png)




2\. Highlight the appropriate denomation to extend.

3\. Click Add. The denomination becomes editable.

4\. Enter the beginning range (up to nine digits) in the Beginning Range column.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="80" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 6 Maintenance Console" -->

5\. Enter the ending range (up to nine digits) in the Ending Range column.

6\. Click OK. Verify the Extended Range for the denomination is Yes.

7\. Click Done. The Extended Denom Range pane shows the new values.


#### 6.8.2 Modifying Extended Denom Ranges

To modify an existing extended denom range:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Maintenance Console > Extended Denom
Range. The Extended Denom Range pane appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/81.1.png)




2\. Highlight the appropriate denom to modify, then click Modify. The denom becomes
editable.

3\. Enter the beginning range (up to nine digits) in the Beginning Range column.

4\. Enter the ending range (up to nine digits) in the Ending Range column.

5\. Click OK. Verify that the changes appear in the Extended Denom Range window.

6\. Click Done. The changes update.


#### 6.8.3 Deleting Extended Denom Ranges

To delete an existing extended denom range:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Maintenance Console > Extended Denom
Range. The Extended Denom Range pane appears.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="81" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 6 Maintenance Console" -->




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/82.1.png)




2\. Highlight the appropriate extended denom to delete.

3\. Click Delete. A confirmation window appears.

4\. Click OK. The confirmation window closes.

5\. Click Done. The Extended Denom Range pane no longer displays the extended denom
range.


## 6.9 Machine Types

Machine type codes assist with entering information for a machine. Type codes group together
machines with the same basic information (manufacturer, denomination, par percentage, and
description). Type codes can be assigned to as many machines as necessary. However, once
assigned, a machine's type code cannot be changed unless the machine is converted to a
different house number.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/82.2.png)




A machine's type code cannot be changed if the machine is newly assigned.

Each type code has a unique number. After a machine receives a type code, the correct
information for the fields automatically populates, thus reducing data entry.

To view machine types:

Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Maintenance Console > Machine Types. The Machine
Types pane appears.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="82" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 6 Maintenance Console" -->

### 6.9.2 Modifying Machine Codes

To modify a type code:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Maintenance Console > Machine Types. The
Machine Types pane appears.

2\. Click Modify in the Machine Types window. The Machine Type Setup Wizard appears.

3\. Edit the fields as needed.

4\. Click Next. The second Machine Type Setup Wizard window appears.

5\. Edit the Meter Tolerance values in the meter fields as needed.

6\. Click Finish. The updated Maintenance Console Machine Types window appears.


### 6.9.3 Deleting Machine Codes

To delete a type code:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Maintenance Console > Machine Types. The
Machine Types pane appears.

2\. Select a machine type code in the Machine Types pane.

3\. Click Delete under the Machine Types pane. The Machine Types pane no longer displays
the machine type.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/87.1.png)




!
No confirmation window appears when deleting a type code. Always confirm the
type code being deleted before selecting the action. If the type code does not delete,
the following message appears: "The value you are trying to delete is referenced by
another table." Type codes cannot be deleted when in use.


## 6.10 Reasons

Use the Reasons options to manage reasons why a task may be completed. For example, a hand
pay can be completed due to a machine malfunction, or a printer jam may requires an FJP
reprint.

To view reasons:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Maintenance Console > Reasons. The Reasons
pane opens.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="87" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 6 Maintenance Console" -->




![Figure 6-8 Maintenance Console-Reasons](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/88.1.png)




## 6.10.1 Adding Reasons


### To add a reason:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Maintenance Console > Reasons. The Reasons
pane opens.

2\. Click Add. The New Reason window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/88.2.png)




3\. Enter the name of the new reason in the Description field. (Must be unique by a minimum
of one character.)

4\. Select a reason type from the Type drop-down list:

· FJP Reprint Comment-Reprinting a ticket

· Hand Pay Comment-Adding a manual hand pay

· Hand Pay Void-Voiding a hand pay

· Manual Ticket Entry-Manually entering a ticket

· W2G Skip-Recording W2G information for jackpots

5\. Click Save. The reason appears on the Reasons pane.


## 6.10.2 Modifying Reasons


### To modify a reason:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Maintenance Console > Reasons. The Reasons
pane opens.

2\. Click Add. The New Reason window appears.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="88" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 6 Maintenance Console" -->

3\. Select a reason to modify in the Reasons pane.

4\. Click Modify. The Update Reason window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/89.1.png)




The Update Reason window is identical to the New Reason window. The only
difference is the name in the title bar.

5\. Edit the reason's Description and Type fields as necessary.

6\. Click Save. The changes save.


## 6.10.3 Deleting Reasons

To delete a reason:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Maintenance Console > Reasons. The Reasons
pane opens.

2\. Select a reason to delete in the Reasons pane.

3\. Click Delete. The Reasons pane no longer displays the deleted reason.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/89.2.png)




!
No confirmation window appears when deleting a reason. Always confirm the
reason being deleted before selecting the action. If the reason does not delete, the
following message appears: "The value you are trying to delete is referenced by
another table." Reasons cannot be deleted when in use.


## 6.11 Authorization Limits

Authorization limits define the monetary value that users may approve in specified
transactions.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/89.3.png)




Maintain the groups to which the limits are tied at User Management Console
\> Groups.

To modify a user group's authorization limits:

1\. Click IGT Spade > Floor.

2\. Click Maintenance Console > Authorization Limits. The Authorization Limits pane
appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/89.4.png)




3\. Select a user group.

4\. Click Modify. The Authorization Limits window appears.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="89" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 6 Maintenance Console" -->




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/90.1.png)




5\. Enter the maximum limits for the user group in the following fields:

· Maximum Fill

· Maximum Jackpot

· Jackpot Override

· Maximum Voucher

· Total Ticket Override

6\. Select a level of paging to which the group belongs in the Page Level field (for alerts).

7\. Click OK. The changes save.

#### 6.12.1 Adding FJP Zones


##### To add a new FJP zone:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Maintenance Console > FJP > Zones. The Zones
pane appears.

2\. Click Add. The FJP Zone Setup window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/92.1.png)




3\. Enter the zone's name in the Description field.

4\. Select Taxable Accrual Enabled to enable taxable accrual for the zone.

5\. Assign machine banks to the zone:

a. Select a machine bank in the Available pane OR press and hold CTRL to select
multiple machine banks.

b. Click Assign. The selected machines are assigned to the zone. They move from the
Available pane to the Assigned pane.

OR

Click Assign All. All machine banks are assigned to the zone. They move from the
Available pane to the Assigned pane.

7\. Remove machine banks from the zone as necessary:

a. Select a machine bank in the Assigned window OR press and hold CTRL to select
multiple machine banks.

b. Click Remove. The selected machine banks are removed from the zone. They move
from the Assigned pane to the Available pane.

OR
Click Remove All. All machine banks are removed from the zone. They move from
the Assigned pane to the Available pane.

8\. Click OK. The zone is added. The updated Zones pane appears.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="92" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 6 Maintenance Console" -->


#### 6.12.2 Modifying FJP Zones

To modify an existing FJP zone:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Maintenance Console > FJP > Zones. The Zones
pane appears.

2\. Select a zone to modify in the Zones pane.

3\. Click Modify. The FJP Zone Setup window appears.

4\. Edit the Description and Bank Assignments fields as necessary.

5\. Click OK. The changes save. The updated Zones pane appears.


#### 6.12.3 Deleting FJP Zones

To delete an FJP zone:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Maintenance Console > FJP > Zones. The Zones
pane appears.

2\. Select a zone to delete in the Zones pane.

3\. Click Delete to delete the zone and return to the updated Zones pane.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/93.1.png)




No confirmation window appears when deleting a zone. Always confirm the zone
being deleted before selecting the action. If the zone does not delete, the following
message appears: "The value you are trying to delete is referenced by another
table." A zone in use cannot be deleted.


#### 6.12.4 Adding FJP Stations

To add a new FJP station:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Maintenance Console > FJP > Stations. The
Stations pane appears.

2\. Click Add. The FJP Stations Setup window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/93.2.png)




<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="93" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 6 Maintenance Console" -->

3\. Enter the station's number in the Station Number field.

4\. Enter the station's namein the Description field.

5\. Select Taxable Accrual Enabled to enable taxable accrual for the station.

6\. To assign zones to the new station:

a. Select a zone to assign to a station in the Available pane, or press and hold CTRL to
select multiple zones.

b. Click Assign. The selected zones are assigned to the station.
OR
Click Assign All. All zones are assigned to the station.

7\. To remove zones from the new station:

a. Select a zone to remove from a station in the Assigned window, or press and hold
CTRL to select multiple zones.

b. Click Remove. The selected zones are removed from the station.
OR

Click Remove All. All zones are removed from the station.

8\. Click OK. The new station is created, and the updated Stations pane appears.


## 6.12.5 Modifying FJP Stations

Stations pane appears.

2\. Select a station to modify in the Stations pane.

3\. Click Modify. The FJP Station Setup window appears.

4\. Edit the Description and Zone Assignments fields as necessary.

5\. Click OK. The changes save, and the updated Stations pane appears.

2\. Select a station to delete in the Stations pane.

## 6.13 Fuzzy Jackpot

Fuzzy Jackpots generate excitement on the casino floor by giving the illusion that more large
jackpots are hitting without the required processing for a large jackpot. The Fuzzy Jackpot goes
to the credit meter, and the player can cash out using the regular cash-out process.

The Fuzzy Jackpot feature allows players using a smart card to force an EGM lock-up upon
winning a specified amount (lower than the actual FJP processing limit). This gives players the
perception of winning more often. It also provides the casino more opportunities to interact
with players, as an attendant must reset the EGM (such as in a handpay jackpot situation) in
order for the player to continue.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/95.1.png)




i
Fuzzy Jackpots only work with the Smart Card -- 37 BE2 Code, which houses the
specific instructions to lock up the machine based on the Fuzzy Jackpot
configuration.

Additional functionality includes:

· A self-service option allowing players to reset the EGM if the winning amount is below
a specific amount.

· Configuration of at least five Fuzzy Jackpot groups, with separate limits for each group.

· An Accept button on a NexGen display or self-service window to move Fuzzy Jackpot
credits to the machine.

Limits are defined as follows:

· Limit-Lowest winnings amount to trigger an EGM lock-up.

. Default Limit-Locks the game at that limit setting (as long as the game is not already
in one of the Denom Pairs).

· Denom Limit-Allows for setting up different denoms and locking up at different
amounts. (For example, a denom of .01 could be set to lock up at $100, and .05 could
be set to lock up at $250.) These denoms are referred to as Denom Pairs. Denom limits
cannot be greater than the Self-Service Limit, which cannot be greater than the taxable
or lock-up limit.

· Self-Service Limit-Highest winning amount for which the player can reset a Fuzzy
Jackpot. Setting this limit allows players to reset a locked EGM with winning amounts
between the Limit and the Self-Service Limit. If the Self-Service Limit is employed, an
attendant must reset Fuzzy Jackpot winning amounts between the Self-Service Limit
and the Jackpot Limit.

· Jackpot Limit-Lowest winnings amount at which an attendant must pay a traditional
jackpot and reset the EGM. (Not determined by the Fuzzy Jackpot feature.)


## 6.13.1 Additional Fuzzy Jackpots Information

The following list provides more information about Fuzzy Jackpots:

· Fuzzy Jackpot options only appear on the user interface after configuration in the
Machine Accounting database.

· Fuzzy Jackpot parameters take effect immediately (based on the system).

· Fuzzy Jackpots are not actual jackpots or win increments on the coin-out meter.

· Machines may only belong in one Fuzzy Jackpot group at any given time.

· No reports within Machine Accounting currently display Fuzzy Jackpot information.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="95" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 6 Maintenance Console" -->

· An event is generated for a lockup, which is reset in Advantage Monitor (pay-to-credit
lockup).


## 6.13.2 Adding Fuzzy Jackpot Groups


### To add a Fuzzy Jackpot group:

1\. Navigate to Maintenance Console > System Maintenance. The System Maintenance
pane appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/96.1.png)




## 2. Click Fuzzy Jackpot. The Fuzzy Jackpot pane appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/96.2.png)




<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="96" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 6 Maintenance Console" -->

3\. Click Add. The New Fuzzy Jackpot Group window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/97.1.png)




### Group Information

1\. Enter the name of the Fuzzy Jackpot group in the Name field.

2\. Enter a description of the Fuzzy Jackpot in the Description field.

3\. In the Default Limit field, enter the minimum winnings amount at which the Fuzzy
Jackpot is triggered for ANY denomination not defined in the Denom Limit Pair field
below. Hover over the Default Limit field to view the allowable range. For example, enter
500 to trigger an EGM lock-up for all winnings between 500 units of the denom and the
FJP minimum amount.

4\. In the Self Service Limit field, enter the upper limit for a winnings amount at which
players can reset the EGM without attendant assistance. Hover over the Self-Service Limit
field to view the allowable range.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/97.2.png)




Ť
The Default Limit and the Self Service Limit function independently. Self Service
Limits are mandatory. However, if setting both options, the Default Limit cannot be
set to an amount higher than the Self Service Limit.


### Denom Limit Configuration

1\. Enter a denom in the Denom field.

2\. Enter the limit amount for that denomination in the Limit field.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/97.3.png)




This sets the minimum winnings at which the lock up occurs).

3\. Click Add. The denomination appears in the Denom Limit Par area.

4\. Repeat steps 1 through 3 for each denomination.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="97" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 6 Maintenance Console" -->

![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/98.1.png)

1\. Click Edit Machine. The Machine List window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/98.2.png)




2\. If the applicable EGMs are not listed, use one or more of the following filters to narrow
the results:

· Denomination

· Section

· Bank

· Manufacturer

3\. When the appropriate EGMs are listed:

a. Select individual check boxes for the appropriate EGMs
OR
Click Select All to select all listed EGMs.

b. Click OK. The location codes for the selected EGMs appear in the Machine Location
field of the New Fuzzy Jackpot Group window.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="98" -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 6 Maintenance Console" -->




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/99.1.png)

2\. Verify that the Fuzzy Jackpot pane lists the new Fuzzy Jackpot group.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/99.2.png)




# 6.13.3 Modifying Fuzzy Jackpots

To modify an existing Fuzzy Jackpot group:

1\. Navigate to Maintenance Console > System Maintenance > Fuzzy Jackpot. The
Fuzzy Jackpot pane appears.

# 6.13.4 Deleting Fuzzy Jackpot Groups

To delete an existing Fuzzy Jackpot group:

1\. Navigate to Maintenance Console > System Maintenance > Fuzzy Jackpot. The
Fuzzy Jackpot pane appears.

# 6.14 Scanner

Use the Scanner options to manage scanners at the property. Configure scanner devices within
the system before adding scanners.

To view scanners:

Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Maintenance Console > Scanner > Configurations.
The Configurations pane appears.




![Figure 6-11 Maintenance Console-Scanner Configuration](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/101.2.png)




<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="101" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 6 Maintenance Console" -->


## 6.14.1 Configuring Scanners


### To configure a scanner:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Maintenance Console > Scanner >
Configurations. The Configurations pane appears.

2\. Click Add. The Scanner Configuration window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/102.1.png)




3\. Enter the scanner configuration's name of the device in the Description field.

4\. Enter the barcode prefix for the machine setting in the Machine Prefix field.

5\. Enter the barcode prefix for the cash can setting in the Cash Can Prefix field.

6\. Click Enable Scanner For Manual Meters to allow manual scanning of machine
meters.

7\. Add meters for the scanner.

a. Select a meter from the Available Meters pane.

b. Click >. The meter moves to the Scanner Meters pane.
OR

Click >>. All available meters move to the Scanner Meters pane.

c. To reorder a meter in the Scanned Meters pane, select the meter, then click Up or
Down until it is in the desired placement.

7\. Remove meters from the Scanned Meters pane as necessary:

a. Select a meter in the Scanner Meters window.

b. Click < . The selected meter moves to the Available Meters pane.
OR

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="102" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 6 Maintenance Console" -->

Click <<. All meters move from the Scanner Meters pane to the Available Meters
pane.

8\. Click Save. The device configuration saves, and the Configurations pane reappears.


## 6.14.2 Modifying Scanner Configurations

To modify an existing scanner configuration:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Maintenance Console > Scanner >
Configurations. The Configurations pane appears.

2\. Select a device to modify, then click Modify. The Scanner Configuration window opens.

3\. Modify the information as needed.

4\. Click Save. The configuration changes save, and the Configurations pane reappears.


## 6.14.3 Deleting Scanner Configurations

To delete a scanner configuration:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Maintenance Console > Scanner >
Configurations. The Configurations pane appears.

2\. Select a configuration to delete in the Configurations pane.

3\. Click Delete. The Configurations pane no longer displays the deleted configuration.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/103.1.png)




No confirmation window appears when deleting a scanner configuration. Always
confirm the scanner configuration being deleted before selecting the action. If the
configuration is not deleted, the following message appears: "The value you are
trying to delete is referenced by another table." Scanner configurations cannot be
deleted when in use.

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 6 Maintenance Console" -->

The following table describes the columns of the Paytables pane:


<table>
<tr>
<th>Column</th>
<th>Description</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>PID</td>
<td>System-assigned sequential number identifying a paytable.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Game ID</td>
<td>An identifier populated from the game.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Paytable ID</td>
<td>The actual paytable sent from the game. Characters in excess of six digits appear in the Additional ID field.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Additional ID</td>
<td>Remainder of the Paytable ID if greater than six digits.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Game Num</td>
<td>The order in which this paytable was pulled into the database.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Base%</td>
<td>The specific machine's PAR.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Description</td>
<td>Text description associated with the paytable. Some description information is obtained from the EGM. In this case, data in the Description column populates from the User Description field.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Skill</td>
<td>Y or N-Indicates if the paytable is a game of skill, such as video poker.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Verified</td>
<td>Y or N-Indicates if the paytable data automatically provided by an EGM has been verified.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Status</td>
<td>Indicates if the paytable is available for play (Active) or not available for play (Inactive).</td>
</tr>
</table>


# 6.15.1 Adding Paytables

In most cases, paytable information is provided automatically when an EGM is placed on the
floor. Add a paytable manually if an EGM does not communicate paytable information.

Obtain the data required to identify a paytable from the BASE Program Paytable Cover Sheet or
PAR Sheet provided with the slot machine. Slot technicians should be able to provide this
information.

To add a paytable:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Maintenance Console > Paytables. The Paytables
pane appears.

2\. Click Add. The Paytable Insert window appears.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="104" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 6 Maintenance Console" -->




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/105.1.png)




3\. Enter information in the following fields:

· Game ID-Game ID number

· Paytable ID-Paytable identifier

· Additional ID-Digits in excess of six digits on the Paytable ID

· Game Number-The order in which this paytable is pulled into the database

· Base %-Specific PAR for the game

· Machine Description-Text description associated with the paytable

· User Description-Free-form field; often used to describe a game's theme

Obtain this information from the BASE Program, Paytable Cover Sheet, or Par
Sheet, or as provided by slot technicians.

i

4\. Select Game Of Skill box if the paytable is a poker game.

5\. In the Machine Information pane, select the check boxes for the individual machines
associated with the paytable.

6\. Click Save. The new paytable is created.


# 6.15.2 Modifying Paytables

To modify an existing paytable:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Maintenance Console > Paytables. The Paytables
pane appears.

2\. Select a paytable to modify in the Paytables pane, then click Modify. The Paytable Insert
window appears.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="105" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 6 Maintenance Console" -->

3\. Edit the appropriate fields.

4\. Click Save. The paytable changes save, and the updated Paytables pane reappears.


# 6.15.3 Verifying Paytables




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/106.1.png)




Prior to verifying a paytable, review the information on the main Paytable pane or
from within the Paytable Insert window.

To verify a paytable:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Maintenance Console > Paytables. The Paytables
pane appears.

2\. Select a paytable to verify OR press and hold CTRL to select multiple paytables.

3\. Click Verify. The Verified value for the paytable(s) changes to Y (yes).

Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Maintenance Console > Paytables By Machine. The
Paytables By Machine pane appears.

# 6.16.2 Adding Paytables

To add a paytable:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Maintenance Console > Paytables By Machine.
The Paytables By Machine pane appears.

2\. Click Add. The Paytable Insert window appears.

3\. Enter the Game ID, Paytable ID, Additional ID, Game Number, Base%, Machine
Description and User Description. This information is obtained from the BASE
Program, Paytable Cover Sheet, or Par Sheet, or as provided by the slot technicians.

4\. Select Game Of Skill if the paytable is for a poker game.

5\. Select the check box next to the individual machines associated with the paytable.

6\. Click Save. The paytable appears on the Paytables By Machine tab.


# 6.16.3 Modifying Paytables

To modify an existing paytable:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Maintenance Console > Paytables By Machine.
The Paytables By Machine pane appears.

2\. Select a paytable to modify, then click Modify. The Paytable Insert window opens.

3\. Edit the fields as necessary.

4\. Click Save. The paytable information updates, and the updated Paytables By Machine
pane reappears.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="108" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 6 Maintenance Console" -->


# 6.16.4 Verifying Paytables




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/109.1.png)




Prior to verifying a paytable, review the information on the Paytable By Machine
pane or from within the Paytable Insert window.

To verify a paytable:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Maintenance Console > Paytables By Machine.
The Paytables By Machine pane appears.

2\. Select a paytable to verify OR press and hold CTRL to select multiple paytables.

3\. Click Verify. The Verified field changes to Y (yes).


# 6.17 Tolerance Level




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/109.2.png)




Refer to General Configuration Options on page 34 for more information on
enabling the Tolerance Level optionsIntervals/Periods/Tolerance on page 35

When adding a new machine type, select tolerance levels for it from a drop-down menu. Use
the Tolerance Level settings to manage tolerance levels.




![Figure 6-14 Maintenance Console-Tolerance Level IDs](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/109.3.png)




<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="109" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 6 Maintenance Console" -->


# To add a tolerance level ID:

1\. From the Maintenance Console, click Tolerance Level. The Tolerance Level pane
appears.

2\. Click Add. The Tolerance Level Configuration window appears.

3\. In the Description field, enter a tolerance level description.

4\. Enter a tolerance value for each item in the Meter Name row.

5\. When all rows have a value, click Save. The Tolerance Level list displays the newly
created tolerance level.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/110.1.png)




i
The ID field is automatically assigned upon saving the entry.


## To modify a tolerance level ID:

1\. From the Maintenance Console, click Tolerance Level. The Tolerance Level pane
appears.

2\. Select a Tolerance Level ID.

3\. Click Modify. The Tolerance Level Configuration window appears.

4\. Select a value in the Tolerance Value column, then enter a new value.

5\. When all necessary values have been modified, click Save. The Tolerance Level pane
updates with the new values.


## To delete a tolerance level ID:

1\. From the Maintenance Console, click Tolerance Level. The Tolerance Level pane
appears.

2\. Select a Tolerance Level ID, then click Delete. A message displays: "Delete the selected
task item?"

3\. Click Yes. The item is removed from the Tolerance Level list.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/110.2.png)




The Tolerance Level ID is also removed with the deleted item and is not used again
as more IDs are created.
